text: 11.5g of fat. omega 3 is exactly the kind of fat support you want from a meat. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
0g of carbs. everything else is lackluster. in this category, carbs barely matter.
24.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 370% daily value, vitamin d at 24% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
iron at 16% daily value, zinc at 12% daily value stand out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

on the plus side: small-fish nutrient density is elite. omega-3s, protein, and micronutrients all come together unusually well here. easy to centre a meal around. practical meal value matters. gives a straightforward protein solution. the category often wins on simplicity and reliability.

the drawbacks next: cost and storage can be a pain. practical downsides still count. fat quality can go sideways fast. some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. quality swings a lot by cut and processing. the category can look very different depending on what version you actually buy.
A tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
